Interface ITopologyErrorFeature

Namespace
ESRI.ArcGIS.Geodatabase
Assembly
ESRI.Server.Geodatabase.dll

Provides access to members that return information about topology errors.

[InterfaceType(ComInterfaceType.InterfaceIsIUnknown)]
[Guid("F6EF5C20-B9CC-4BFA-B40D-4F0ABB83F3FA")]
public interface ITopologyErrorFeature

Properties

DestinationClassID

ObjectClassID of the destination feature that created the topology error.

int DestinationClassID { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

DestinationOID

ObjectID of the destination feature that created the topology error.

long DestinationOID { get; }

Property Value

long

Sample Snippet

ErrorID

ID of the error.

long ErrorID { get; }

Property Value

long

Sample Snippet

IsDeleted

Indicates if this topology error feature has been deleted.

bool IsDeleted { get; }

Property Value

bool

IsException

Indicates if topology error is an exception.

bool IsException { get; }

Property Value

bool

Sample Snippet

OriginClassID

ObjectClassID of the origin feature that created the topology error.

int OriginClassID { get; }

Property Value

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

OriginOID

ObjectID of the origin feature that created the topology error.

long OriginOID { get; }

Property Value

long

Examples

Sample Snippet

ShapeType

The geometry type of the topology error.

esriGeometryType ShapeType { get; }

Property Value

esriGeometryType

Examples

Sample Snippet

TopologyRule

The topology rule that caused the topology error.

IRule TopologyRule { get; }

Property Value

IRule

Examples

Sample Snippet

Remarks

<P>The <STRONG>TopologyRule</STRONG> class is used to define the permissible spatial relationships between features within a topology.&nbsp; Topology rules can be defined with a single feature class or between two feature classes.&nbsp; Topology rules can also be defined to the subtype level of a feature class.&nbsp; Topology rules have an origin and a destination feature class, either of which can be set to the subtype level.&nbsp; Depending on the type of topology rule that is implemented, the destination feature class properties may be irrelevant.</P>

TopologyRuleType

The Object Class for the row.

esriTopologyRuleType TopologyRuleType { get; }

Property Value

esriTopologyRuleType

Examples

Sample Snippet
